acceptance_criteria: No persisted artifact or served dashboard response contains raw API keys, common token patterns, private key blocks, configured secret values, raw command text, raw environment values, unsafe absolute paths, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw TASK.md bodies, raw EVAL.md bodies, raw document bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Traversal attempts using relative, absolute, encoded, or symlinked paths cannot read or write outside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots.

Dry-run and non-dry-run produce equivalent redaction, boundary, diagnostic, validation output, planner handoff metadata, git diff redaction behavior, artifact persistence, and safe path label persistence.

Existing TASK.md summary, latest-run summary, next-action summary, Project Docs shortcuts, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, Validation Status dashboard behavior, and run detail Validation Evidence and Compare Previous behavior remains unchanged when Run Artifacts is rendered on run detail views.
Dashboard root shows a compact Active Run section when at least one valid guarded non-terminal run exists.
Each active-run item exposes only sanitized run ID label, status, provider or result label, evaluation state when available, timestamp label, and guarded links to existing run views.
Active-run selection is deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied.

No-active, missing metadata, denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent run metadata renders deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states without server errors.
Dashboard root shows a compact Validation Status section when at least one valid guarded completed run has validation metadata.
Each validation-status item exposes only sanitized run ID label, validation state, safe count labels, timestamp label, and guarded links to existing run views.
Validation-status selection is deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied.

No-validation, missing metadata, denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent validation metadata renders deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states without server errors.
Existing guarded run detail views show a compact Validation Evidence section for a completed run with validation metadata.

Each Validation Evidence section exposes only sanitized run ID label, validation state, safe count labels, timestamp label, sanitized validation step or category labels, and guarded links to existing safe views.
Runs with no validation metadata render deterministic none or unavailable validation evidence without server errors.

Denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent validation evidence metadata renders deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or none states without unsafe reflection.
Guarded run detail shows a compact Compare Previous action when a deterministic previous valid guarded run exists.
The compare action exposes only sanitized current run label, sanitized previous run label, and a guarded link to the existing comparison view.
Runs with no previous comparable run render a deterministic none or unavailable state without server errors.
Previous-run selection is deterministic for missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied timestamps using sanitized stable tie-breakers only.
Existing guarded run detail views show a compact Run Artifacts section for a valid guarded run using fixed allowlisted artifact categories and safe availability labels.
Available Run Artifacts items link only to existing guarded safe views or existing sanitized artifact routes.
Runs with no artifact metadata render deterministic none or unavailable artifact inventory without server errors.

Denied, malformed, stale, partial, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent artifact metadata never renders unsafe labels, raw paths, raw manifests, raw diffs, raw commands, raw environment values, validation payloads, or artifact bodies.
Run Artifacts selection and ordering are deterministic and use fixed category ordering rather than arbitrary file enumeration.

Active Run, Validation Status, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, and Run Artifacts data are derived only from shared sanitized run DTO/helper output, with no handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, validation output, EVAL.md body, document body, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.

No Active Run, Validation Status, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, or Run Artifacts response contains raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw command text, raw environment values, validation payloads, stdout/stderr, diff bodies, unsafe absolute paths, denied payloads, token-like values, configured secrets, private key blocks, omission placeholders, or raw document bodies.

Focused Go serve/dashboard tests cover validation evidence passed, failed, skipped or partial, no metadata, malformed metadata, denied run, stale metadata, hostile-label, token-like values, deterministic labels, and unchanged existing dashboard sections.

Focused Go serve/dashboard tests cover compare-to-previous present, absent, malformed metadata, denied run, tied timestamps, hostile labels, token-like run IDs, deterministic labels, and unchanged existing dashboard sections.

Focused Go serve/dashboard tests cover Run Artifacts present, missing, malformed metadata, denied run, stale metadata, hostile-label, token-like values, deterministic ordering, and unchanged Validation Evidence and Compare Previous sections.
TASK-0050 validation passes focused serve/dashboard tests, go test ./internal/serve, and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.
TASK-0051 validation passes focused serve/dashboard tests, go test ./internal/serve, and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.
TASK-0052 validation passes focused serve/dashboard tests, go test ./internal/serve, and scripts/validate.sh with sanitized release-gate metadata.

Completed security guardrails remain closed after TASK-0052 because focused serve/dashboard tests, go test ./internal/serve, and scripts/validate.sh passed and no current release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence shows a concrete regression.
goals: Generate concrete specs and tasks from a single plan file while preserving an auditable run history.

Keep generated artifacts, served files, plan inputs, workspace mutations, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, dashboard summaries, active-run progress, validation status, run detail validation evidence, compare-to-previous links, run artifact inventory, and run inspection surfaces inside the resolved workspace or guarded run artifact roots.

Prevent secrets, token-like values, private key material, omission placeholders, raw commands, raw environment values, unsafe paths, raw manifests, raw artifact bodies, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw TASK.md bodies, raw EVAL.md bodies, raw document bodies, and attacker-controlled denied payloads from being persisted or rendered.

Provide a dashboard-first local jj serve experience with guarded run detail, history, comparison, sanitized audit export views, compact sanitized TASK.md work-queue summary, latest-run summary, next-action summary, Project Docs shortcuts, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run progress, Validation Status on the dashboard root, compact validation evidence on existing run detail views, guarded compare-to-previous navigation from run detail views, and compact sanitized run artifact inventory on run detail views.
Preserve dry-run and non-dry-run workflows with identical redaction, path-boundary, diagnostics, artifact persistence, diff redaction, validation, and planner handoff metadata behavior.
Ensure auto and balanced task proposal resolution selects bugfix or security only from concrete current evidence, not completed guardrails, healthy context, or absent evidence.
Use scripts/validate.sh as the single documented release validation entry point for local and CI security release gates.
Keep completed security guardrails closed unless a concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary regression is observed.
non_goals: Provide a multi-user dashboard or authentication system.
Replace git review or guarantee AI output correctness.

Expose raw commands, environment dumps, secret values, sensitive absolute paths, private key material, token-like strings, omission placeholders, raw artifact bodies, raw manifests, raw diff bodies, validation output payloads, planner handoff payloads, raw prompt exports, raw TASK.md bodies, raw EVAL.md bodies, raw document bodies, or attacker-controlled denied path payloads.
Add raw manifest export, raw artifact export, raw diff export, raw prompt export, artifact content bundling, raw artifact upload, raw manifest upload, or raw diff upload.

Add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, broad file import behavior, scanners, pagination, configurable dashboard limits, configurable recent-run limits, configurable active-run limits, validation execution controls, or unrelated user-facing behavior as part of dashboard summary, run detail validation evidence, compare-to-previous work, or run artifact inventory work.
Allow explicitly provided plan files outside the workspace by default.
Reopen completed tasks without fresh failing validation, tests, CI, confirmed disclosure, or boundary regression evidence.
Expose child command stdout or stderr from the release validation entry point or CI wrapper.
requirements: jj run must read a non-empty Markdown plan from the explicitly provided path using documented path resolution behavior, then require the resolved plan file to remain inside the configured workspace boundary after canonicalization and symlink evaluation.

Rejected external, traversal, absolute-escape, encoded-escape, symlink-escape, unreadable, malformed, or secret-looking plan inputs must produce deterministic sanitized errors before content read or artifact persistence.

Accepted plan content, planner output, Codex output, command summaries, evaluation output, validation output, docs writes, manifest serialization, event writes, run artifact writes, git diff artifacts, dashboard DTOs, and rendered dashboard data must pass through shared redaction before persistence or display.

Manifest and general JSON persistence must use safe workspace, run, relative, or generic path labels and must not store raw workspace, plan, external config, git, repository, validation command, or baseline paths.
Command execution records must persist sanitized argv, cwd labels, status, duration, and result metadata only; raw command text and raw environment values must not be stored or rendered.

Validation stdout and stderr artifacts and git diff artifacts must be sanitized before persistence; evidence reports may include only booleans, counts, sanitized category labels, and safe relative artifact labels.

Dry-run and non-dry-run modes must apply identical plan path validation, redaction, path-boundary, artifact persistence, git diff capture, diff redaction, manifest metadata, diagnostics, validation output sanitization, planner handoff sanitization, and denial behavior.

jj serve must only serve files from guarded workspace and run artifact roots and must reject traversal, absolute, encoded, secret-looking, token-like, duplicate hostile query, or symlink escape inputs with deterministic denied or unavailable states.
Run IDs may contain only safe letters, numbers, dots, underscores, and dashes, must not use traversal-like forms, and must be rejected when they match configured secrets or common token patterns.

Run detail, history, comparison, audit export, dashboard latest-run, next-action, Project Docs, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, Validation Status, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, and Run Artifacts handlers must obtain data through shared sanitized DTO construction or shared helpers, not handler-local raw manifest, artifact, command, environment, diff, validation output, EVAL.md body, document body, or filesystem reads after route input parsing.

Untrusted, stale, partial, malformed, manually edited, or internally inconsistent manifest fields, artifact references, validation summaries, command metadata, diff metadata, provider fields, timestamps, evaluation metadata, findings, active-run metadata, validation-status metadata, validation-evidence metadata, compare-previous metadata, run-artifact metadata, and next-action hints must render only deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, all-clear, none, or sanitized states.

The dashboard root must preserve existing sanitized TASK.md summary, latest-run summary, next-action summary, fixed Project Docs shortcuts, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run, and Validation Status behavior.
The dashboard root must show a compact Active Run section when at least one valid guarded non-terminal run exists, using only shared sanitized run DTO or helper output.
Each Active Run item must expose only sanitized run ID label, status, provider or result label, evaluation state when available, timestamp label, and guarded links to existing run views.
Active Run selection must be deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied, using sanitized stable tie-breakers only.

No-active, missing metadata, denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent active-run metadata must render deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states without server errors or unsafe reflection.
The dashboard root must show a compact Validation Status section when at least one valid guarded completed run has validation metadata, using only shared sanitized run DTO or helper output.
Each Validation Status item must expose only sanitized run ID label, validation state, safe count labels when available, timestamp label, and guarded links to existing run views.
Validation Status selection must be deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied, using sanitized stable tie-breakers only.

No-validation, missing metadata, denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent validation metadata must render deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states without server errors or unsafe reflection.
Existing guarded run detail views must show a compact Validation Evidence section when the selected guarded completed run has validation metadata.

The Validation Evidence section must expose only sanitized run ID label, validation state, safe pass/fail/skip/error count labels when available, timestamp label, sanitized validation step or category labels, and guarded links to existing safe views.

Missing, denied, malformed, partial, stale, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent validation evidence metadata must render deterministic none, unavailable, unknown, or denied states without server errors or unsafe reflection.
Existing guarded run detail views must show a compact Compare Previous action when a deterministic previous valid guarded run exists.
The Compare Previous action must expose only sanitized current run label, sanitized previous run label, and a guarded link to the existing comparison view.
Runs with no previous comparable run must render a deterministic none or unavailable state without server errors.
Previous-run selection must be deterministic when timestamps are missing, malformed, duplicated, or tied, using sanitized stable tie-breakers only.
Existing guarded run detail views must show a compact Run Artifacts section for valid guarded runs.

The Run Artifacts section must list only fixed expected artifact categories and safe availability labels, such as input plan, generated SPEC, generated TASK, evaluation, manifest summary, git diff summary, and Codex summary when present.
Available Run Artifacts items must link only to existing guarded safe views or existing sanitized artifact routes.
Runs with no artifact metadata must render a deterministic none or unavailable state without server errors.

Denied, malformed, stale, partial, hostile-label, token-like, or internally inconsistent artifact metadata must render deterministic denied, unknown, unavailable, or none states without unsafe reflection.
Run Artifacts data must be derived from shared sanitized run DTO/helper output and must not enumerate arbitrary files or add broad file browsing behavior.

Missing, malformed, denied, stale, hostile-label, token-like, partial, or internally inconsistent run metadata must not render unsafe labels, raw paths, raw manifests, raw diffs, raw commands, raw environment values, validation payloads, or artifact bodies.

Active Run, Validation Status, Validation Evidence, Compare Previous, and Run Artifacts rendering must not add new dashboard pages, CLI flags, raw export endpoints, scanners, artifact uploads, broad file browsing, pagination, configurable limits, or unrelated user-facing features.

Auto or balanced task proposal resolution must choose bugfix only from positive current blocker evidence and security only from concrete release-gate, CI, validation, test, disclosure, or boundary evidence showing a security or privacy regression.

scripts/validate.sh must remain the single documented release validation entry point, run focused security boundary suites, full tests, vet, CLI build, and diff-check, suppress child command output, emit only deterministic sanitized metadata, and exit non-zero on any failing step.

Completed security guardrails must remain closed unless scripts/validate.sh, focused Go security suites, full tests, vet, build, diff-check, or the CI release gate produces a concrete disclosure, path-boundary, artifact, dashboard, planner handoff, validation output, git diff, command metadata, or release-gate output regression.
summary: jj is a CLI that turns a planning file into implementation-ready specs, tasks, Codex runs, validation artifacts, and a local dashboard. Security remains the controlling constraint: generated artifacts, served run-inspection surfaces, explicit plan inputs, command metadata, manifests, validation outputs, git diff artifacts, planner and Codex handoff metadata, run metadata, dashboard summaries, project document shortcuts, recent runs, evaluation findings, active-run progress, validation status, run detail validation evidence, compare-to-previous links, sanitized run artifact inventory, and raw document handling must be guarded, redacted, and kept inside the configured workspace or allowed run artifact roots. Completed security guardrails remain closed unless concrete validation, test, CI, disclosure, or boundary evidence proves a regression. The dashboard summarizes TASK.md, latest run, next action, Project Docs, Recent Runs, Evaluation Findings, Active Run progress, compact sanitized validation status, guarded validation evidence, compare-to-previous navigation, and a compact sanitized artifact inventory on run detail views without exposing raw artifacts.
title: jj Security Boundary and Dashboard Run Inspection
